Affordable housing statement: A statutory document defining how a proposed development satisfies national (NPPF) and local requirements for affordable housing provision. It quantifies proportion, tenure mix (social rent, shared ownership), unit schedules, and viability justifications. Key sections: Policy Context, Unit Schedule, Financial Viability Assessment, Section 106 obligations.
Air quality assessment: A technical environmental report evaluating development impacts on ambient air quality (NO2, PM10). It determines compliance with statutory air quality objectives and human health standards. Key sections: Dispersion Modelling, Emission Sources, AQMA compliance, Mitigation Measures (dust management).
Biodiversity survey and report: An ecological assessment identifying habitats, protected species, and ecological networks. Its purpose is to evaluate statutory protection compliance and Biodiversity Net Gain (BNG) requirements. Key sections: Phase 1 Habitat Survey, BNG Calculations, Mitigation Hierarchy, Ecological Enhancement.
CIL: A statutory financial declaration (Community Infrastructure Levy) used to calculate levy liability under CIL Regulations 2010. It determines chargeable development area and infrastructure contributions. Key sections: Net Additional Floorspace, Relief Claims, Ownership Certificates.
Coal mining risk assessment: A geotechnical report for developments in coalfield areas identifying historic mining risks such as ground instability and gas emissions. It assesses likelihood of subsidence and mine entries. Key sections: Mining Legacy Review, Coal Authority Data, Remediation Strategy.
Daylight or Sunlight assessment: A technical analysis evaluates natural light impact on surrounding properties and internal amenity based on BRE Guidelines. Key sections: Vertical Sky Component (VSC), Annual Probable Sunlight Hours (APSH), 3D modelling, Sensitive Receptor Analysis.
Design and Access Statement: A mandatory document explaining design rationale and inclusive access strategies. It demonstrates response to site context and design policy. Key sections: Site Analysis, Design Principles (scale, layout), Access Strategy, Policy Compliance.
Environmental statement: A comprehensive document for Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for high-impact developments. Systematically predicts and mitigates effects across multiple topics. Key sections: Non-Technical Summary, Cumulative Impacts, Residual Effects, Topic Chapters (Noise, Air, Ecology).
Fire Statements: A specialist fire safety document required for high-rise developments under the Building Safety Act 2022. It outlines fire strategy principles and means of escape. Key sections: Means of Escape, Fire Service Access, Water Supply, External Wall Systems.
Flood risk assessment: An evaluation of flooding risk (fluvial, pluvial, tidal) to and from a development. Ensures development is safe and appropriately located per national policy. Key sections: Sequential Test, Exception Test, Hydraulic Modelling, SuDS Strategy.
Heritage Statement: An assessment of impact on designated/non-designated heritage assets (listed buildings, conservation areas). Identifies significance and justifies changes. Key sections: Significance Assessment, Direct/Setting Impact, Statutory Duty Compliance.
Land contamination assessment: A phased report identifying risks from previous land uses to human health or the environment. Key sections: Desk Study, Site Investigation (Phase 1-3), Risk Assessment, Remediation Strategy.
Lighting assessment: Evaluation of artificial lighting impact, focusing on safety, glare, light spill, and ecological disturbance. References standard illumination levels. Key sections: Baseline Conditions, Lighting Design, Receptor Impact Assessment.
Noise Impact Assessment: A technical report evaluating effects of noise from or affecting a development to protect amenity. Key sections: Baseline Acoustic Survey, Noise Modelling, Acoustic Insulation, Mitigation Measures.
Planning Statement: A core document providing the policy justification for approval. Argues the 'Planning Balance' based on local and national policy. Key sections: Site Description, Proposal Details, Policy Analysis, Planning Balance.
Site waste management plan: A plan outlining construction/demolition waste management, focusing on recycling and statutory compliance. Key sections: Waste Types, Management Strategies, Monitoring/Audit Trail.
Statement of Community Involvement: A document recording stakeholder and community engagement undertaken prior to application submission. Key sections: Engagement Methods, Feedback Summary, Design Responses.
Structural Survey: Condition and stability assessment of existing buildings to confirm suitability for proposed works. Key sections: Inspection Findings, Structural Analysis, Recommendations.
SuDS Strategy: A Sustainable Drainage Systems plan focused on managing surface water runoff and water quality. Key sections: Drainage Design, Storage Features, Climate Change Allowance.
Sustainability Appraisal: Evaluation of environmental, social, and economic impacts to demonstrate sustainable development compliance. Key sections: Assessment Framework, Impact Analysis, Mitigation.
Transport Assessment: Technical evaluation of development impact on transport networks and safety. Key sections: Traffic Analysis, Junction Capacity, Accessibility, Trip Generation.
Travel Plan: Strategy to promote sustainable transport and reduce reliance on private vehicles. Key sections: Behavioural Objectives, Targets, Monitoring, Action Plan.
Tree Survey and Arboricultural Report: Assessment of tree condition, retention, and protection on or near a site. Key sections: Tree Inventory, Root Protection Areas (RPA), Protection Measures.
